breakdown: Stem of codeti, to urge, exhort, reprove.
Past participle suffix: having been exhorted.

cuti.meaning: vanishing; passing away; death (the fall from one existence)
breakdown: Root meaning to fall away, to pass from one state of existence.
Abstract noun suffix: the act of falling away, vanishing.

cāgo.meaning: abandoning; forsaking; relinquishment; generosity of letting go
breakdown: Root meaning to give up, to abandon (strengthened to cāg-).

cānujaññā.meaning: and may permit; and would approve, consent to
cānujaññā.summary: A sandhi fusion of the particle ca with the optative of anujānāti.
cānujaññā.gram: particle + verb, 3rd person singular optative of anujānāti (anu + √ñā)
breakdown: And (the c- of the fused form).
Optative of anujānāti, to permit, approve, consent to (anu + √ñā). Sandhi: ca + anu- > cānu-.

cāsāradassino.gram: compound adjective, masculine plural, fused with the particle ca
breakdown: Without essence; the nonessential (a- negative + sāra, essence, core).
Seeing, those who see (from √dis, to see).

cātuddasī.meaning: the fourteenth day of the fortnight
cātuddasī.summary: Named with pañcadasī and aṭṭhamī: the 14th, 15th, and 8th days of the lunar fortnight, the traditional uposatha observance days.
breakdown: Feminine suffix forming the ordinal day-name.

cātuddasī pañcadasī.meaning: the fourteenth and fifteenth (days of the lunar fortnight)
cātuddasī pañcadasī.summary: Together with the eighth day (aṭṭhamī), these are the traditional uposatha observance days of the fortnight.
breakdown: The fifteenth day, full-moon or new-moon day (pañca + dasa + ī).

cātumāsiniyā.meaning: of the four-month period; belonging to the four months (of a season)
breakdown: Feminine possessive suffix, here in oblique case.
Restraint; a watch (division) of the night; here the fourfold discipline.
Oblique case ending (instrumental, dative, genitive, or locative singular).

cūḷahatthipadopamasuttaṁ.meaning: the Cūḷahatthipadopama Sutta, the Shorter Discourse on the Simile of the Elephant's Footprint
cūḷahatthipadopamasuttaṁ.summary: This is a compound sutta title meaning "the shorter discourse with the simile of the elephant's footprint".

dakkhiṇeyyo.meaning: worthy of offerings; worthy of donations
dakkhiṇeyyo.summary: Said of those, such as the Saṅgha, to whom gifts are fittingly given.
dakkhiṇeyyo.gram: adjective, masculine nominative singular, from dakkhiṇā
breakdown: Offering, donation, gift given with respect (√dakkh).
Suffix of worthiness: deserving of, fit to receive.

dakkhiṇeyyo worthy of.summary: A fragmentary lexicon row; the headword proper is dakkhiṇeyyo, worthy of offerings.
dakkhiṇeyyo worthy of.note: uncertain: the key is an artifact of a split vocabulary row ("dakkhiṇeyyo worthy of / offerings"); the entry belongs under dakkhiṇeyyo.

dakkhiṇeyyā.meaning: worthy of donations; worthy of offerings (plural)
dakkhiṇeyyā.gram: adjective, masculine nominative plural, from dakkhiṇā
breakdown: Suffix of worthiness: deserving of, fit to receive.
Root associated with skill and the right-hand side.

dakkhiṇā.meaning: offering; donation; gift given with respect
breakdown: Root associated with skill, worthiness, the right hand.
Feminine noun suffix: a respectful offering.

dalidde.gram: adjective, locative singular or masculine accusative plural

daliddā.gram: adjective, plural or feminine form of dalidda

damasaccena.meaning: by self-control and truth; through restraint and truthfulness
breakdown: Instrumental singular ending: by means of.

danapūjanāsu:.meaning: in gain, honour, respect, reverence, esteem and veneration from others
danapūjanāsu:.summary: A fragment of the longer compound paralābhasakkāragarukāramānanavandanapūjanāsu, listing the worldly rewards a person may receive from others.
danapūjanāsu:.note: uncertain: the key is a truncated tail of the full compound; the initial element is vandana + pūjanā, not dana.

dandhā.gram: adjective (√dandh + a), plural or feminine form
breakdown: Root meaning to be slow, sluggish.

dasabalassa sāsanaṁ.meaning: the teaching of the One of Ten Powers (an epithet of the Buddha)
dasabalassa sāsanaṁ.summary: A phrase meaning literally "the teaching of the one possessing ten powers", dasabala being an epithet of the Tathāgata.
breakdown: Power, strength; genitive singular: of the one of (ten) powers.
dasabalassa sāsanaṁ.goenka: Dasabala, "the one of ten powers", is an epithet of the Tathāgata. The ten powers are ten fields of perfect knowledge: he understands according to reality (yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti) what is possible and what is impossible; the results of past, present and future actions with regard to their bases and causes; where any path of practice leads — to the hell realm, animal realm, ghost realm, human realm, deva realm, or to nibbāna; the world with its numerous and diverse elements; the diverse dispositions of beings; the range of faculties of other beings and persons; the defilement and purification associated with the jhānas, liberations, concentrations and attainments; and the remaining powers of recollecting past lives, the divine eye, and the destruction of the taints. The phrase dasabalassa sāsanaṁ thus means the dispensation of the Buddha so endowed.

dassana-sampadāya.meaning: by the attainment of insight; through accomplishment in seeing

dassanīyo.meaning: fair to behold; beautiful; worth seeing
dassanīyo.gram: potential participle of √dis (to see), masculine nominative singular
breakdown: Potential participle suffix: worthy of, fit to be.

dassenti.gram: verb, 3rd person plural present, causative of passati (√dis)
breakdown: Causative stem of √dis, to see: to cause to see, to show.

dassesuṁ.gram: verb, 3rd person plural aorist of dasseti (causative of √dis)
breakdown: Causative stem of √dis, to see: to show.

dassāmannañca.summary: A sandhi fusion of three words: dassāmi (I will give) + annaṁ (food) + ca (and).
dassāmannañca.gram: verb phrase fused by sandhi; future 1st person singular + accusative noun + particle
breakdown: I will give (future of dadāti, √dā). Final -i elided before annaṁ.
Food, boiled rice (accusative). The -ṁ assimilates to -ñ before ca.
Entered upon, having attained (past participle of āpajjati): one who has entered into compassion.

daḷhaparakkamo.meaning: of steadfast exertion; firm in energy

daḷhaparakkamā.meaning: of steadfast exertion; firm in effort and energy (plural)

daḷhaparakkamā:.meaning: strong and firm in exertion, effort, energy
daḷhaparakkamā:.gram: compound adjective, masculine nominative plural (see daḷhaparakkamā)
daḷhaparakkamā:.note: uncertain: key duplicates daḷhaparakkamā with a stray colon from the source vocabulary row.

daḷhāya.gram: past participle of √dah used as adjective, dative/genitive singular
breakdown: Root meaning to be firm; √dah + ta > daḍha > daḷha.

daṇḍena.meaning: by a stick; with a rod

daṭṭhu.meaning: having seen; to see, observe, behold
breakdown: Root meaning to see (with the dakkh- present stem).

desessāmi.meaning: I will teach; I shall expound
desessāmi.gram: verb, 1st person singular future of deseti (causative of √dis, to point out)
breakdown: Stem of deseti, to teach, expound (causative of disati).

desetabbaṁ.meaning: should be taught; to be expounded
desetabbaṁ.gram: potential (future passive) participle of deseti, neuter nominative singular
breakdown: Stem of deseti, to teach, expound.
Potential participle suffix: that which should be done.
Stem of deseti, to teach, expound.

desetā.gram: masculine agent noun (desetar), nominative singular, from deseti
breakdown: Stem of deseti, to teach, expound.
Agent noun suffix: the one who teaches.
Stem of dessati, to hate, detest.

devamanussānaṁ.meaning: of gods and humans; for devas and mankind

devamanussānaṁ nibbedhabhāgiyavāsanābhāg.meaning: "the giver to gods and humans of the excellent Dhamma that partakes of penetration and of (good) latent impressions" (an epithet of the Buddha, with quotation marker ti)
devamanussānaṁ nibbedhabhāgiyavāsanābhāg.gram: phrase; dative/genitive plural noun + long compound epithet + quotation particle
breakdown: Of/for gods and humans (deva + manussa, dative/genitive plural).
Quotation marker (iti); final -ī + ti by sandhi.

devatā.meaning: deity; god or goddess; celestial being
devatā.gram: feminine noun, from deva (√div + a + tā)
breakdown: Suffix forming a general or collective noun: divinity, deity.
devatā.goenka: Devatā is a general expression for gods, female or male. They dwell above the human realm, beginning with the realm of the four great kings (cātumahārājikā devā), the sphere of the thirty-three (tāvatiṁsā devā), the field of the yāmā devā, the tusitā devā, those who delight in creation (nimmānaratī devā), and those dwelling in the sphere of beings with power over the creations of others (paranimmitavasavattī devā).

dhajaggasuttaṁ.meaning: the Dhajagga Sutta, the Discourse on the Top of the Banner

dhajāhatā.meaning: struck down under the banner; taken as booty in battle
breakdown: Struck, beaten (past participle of āhanati): captured under the standard, taken as spoils.

dhamma.meaning: Dhamma: the teaching of the Buddha; the universal law of nature; the path. dhamma: phenomenon; mental object; quality; thing
dhamma.summary: Capitalised Dhamma and lowercase dhamma are distinguished senses of the same word.
breakdown: Root meaning to hold, uphold, support: that which upholds.
dhamma.goenka: Capitalisation carries the distinction. Dhamma (capitalised) denotes the teaching of the Buddha, the universal law of nature, and the path itself; dhamma (lowercase) denotes a phenomenon, a mental object, an element of experience. Klaus uses Dhamma capitalised throughout to denote both the Buddha's teaching and the universal law. He explicitly distinguishes saddhamma, the true Dhamma, which contains seven qualities: saddhā (faith), sati (awareness), hirī (sense of shame), ottappa (moral sense of guilt), bahusacca (ample learning), vīriya (diligence) and paññā (wisdom). The phrase saddhammassavanaṁ, listening to the true Dhamma, is presented as one of the rarities (dullabho) — encountering the teaching is itself rare. Volume 2's title, Svākkhāto ca Parisuddho Dhammo, means "So Well-Explained and Utterly Pure Dhamma". The Pāli term is retained in tradition usage; it is not replaced by the Sanskrit dharma.

dhammacakkappavattanasuttaṁ.meaning: the Dhammacakkappavattana Sutta, the Discourse Setting in Motion the Wheel of Dhamma
breakdown: Setting in motion, turning (the doubled pp is sandhi doubling).

dhammacariyasamacariyatthā.meaning: for the sake of walking in Dhamma and walking rightly; for the purpose of righteous and correct conduct
dhammacariyasamacariyatthā.gram: compound noun, ablative/dative sense (for the purpose of)
breakdown: Correct walking, right conduct (sama, even/right + cariya).
For the purpose of, for the sake of (attha, aim, benefit).

dhammaniruttipaṭisambhidāvisayato.meaning: by way of the analytical knowledge of Dhamma and of language; concerning the characteristic field of the analyses of Dhamma and grammatical exegesis
breakdown: Ablative suffix: by way of, with respect to.

dhammasandosā.meaning: corruption of the Dhamma; defilement, pollution of the teaching
dhammasandosā.gram: masculine compound noun, nominative plural or ablative singular
